_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Pain followed by progressive neurological deficits
Upper cervical and foramen magnum tumors: suboccipital pain, distal hand weakness with atrophy of hand muscles
Thoracic lesions: long tract signs with corticospinal involvement, lower limb weakness and spasticity; sensory ataxia; late bowel and bladder involvement
Filum ependymoma: low back pain, asymmetrical radicular pain to both lower limb, pain on recumbency

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes contain detailed history, signs & symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of treatment?
History, physical examination, and radiological investigations confirm the diagnosis?
Clinical presentation and radiological investigations indicative of surgery?

cpd_claim.q: Detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details provided?
Discharge summary with follow-up advise submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes missing detailed history, signs & symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, or planned line of treatment.
History, physical examination, or radiological investigations fail to confirm the diagnosis.
Clinical presentation or radiological investigations are not indicative of surgery.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) lacking daily vitals or treatment details.
Post-op CT Spine report not submitted.

clinical_key_pointers: Intradural extramedullary spinal cord tumors (IESCT) constitute approximately two thirds of spine tumors.
The most commonly encountered types of IESCT are schwannomas and meningiomas, with myxopapillary ependymomas encountered less frequently.
surgical_options: Aim is total excision of tumor
Aggressive filum terminale ependymoma may require postoperative radiotherapy
